free_text: 12C(gamma,p)11B cross section from 80 to 157 MeV.
Electron tagged bremsstrahlung.
Scintillator.
Each spectrometer consisted of 2 thin de-layers
consisting of plastic scintillators.
A natural graphite  target  of  thickness  152.5(0.8)
mg/cm**2 was positioned at 30 degree with respect to
the beam.
Photoproton energy    spectra   were   measured   and
converted to the functions  of  residual  excitation
energy  E-exc  of  final  nucleus  11B.  In order to
estimate the cross  section  leading  to  the  final
nucleus  states gaussian fitting  procedure was used
for the peaks which are evident in  the  photoproton
spectra   and   corresponding   to   population   of
low-lying states of 11B at excitation energies  near
E-exc  = 0,  7,  and 13 MeV.  The gaussian centroids
were restricted to the energy ranges E-exc  =  (0  -
No attempt was made to determine  the  cross  section
for   the  higher  regions  of  E-exc,  but  further
gaussians were fitted at E-exc = 17 - 18,  21  -  23
MeV,  together with a broader gaussian above E-exc =
45 MeV,  and quasideutron  continuum,  in  order  to
provide a good overall fit to photoproton spectra.
Mean-root-square uncertainties  presented.
Systematic uncertainties are estimated  to be
The 12C(g,p)11B differential cross section  has  been
measured  over  proton angles ranging from 58 to 128
degrees,  using tagged photons of energy  80  -  157
MeV,  for  low-lying  regions of residual excitation
energy in 11B. The data have been compared with four
different  types  of  calculation.  It is shown that
scaling of the cross section with momentum  mismatch
occurs for both ground-state and excited state data.

lower case.
